title: terpsichore pichinchensis
intro: Terpsichore pichinchensis, also known as Pichinchensis fern, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae, native to Ecuador and Peru in South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Terpsichore pichinchensis include its ability to soothe coughs and reduce congestion, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and protect against cell damage. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some cases, it may also be used in the form of a poultice or applied topically to the affected area.
